decision-report.diff-summary: Session working on branch claude/zen-knuth-jJnLZ, base ref 0470103 (main), 0 commits ahead. At report time: 0 files committed, 3 unstaged edits to pre-existing buffers, and 13 untracked new files introducing the decision tooling — refs/ XML spec + README + out/ dir, .claude/skills/git-platform-eval/ skill with 3 TS scripts, and the claude-code-agents-typescript/src/agents/platform-eval/ Opus 4.7 Agent SDK evaluator (index.ts, cli.ts, xml-spec.ts, diff-reader.ts, deterministic-checks.ts, eval-loop.ts). No modifications to existing subrepos' source; package.json adds the agent-sdk dep. All new code additive; commit will move these from "untracked" to "committed" before PR.

criterion.rationale: code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web.md references GitHub PRs/issues as first-class targets; @claude mentions on PRs are a GitHub-native flow. No equivalent surface documented for GitLab MRs at this time.
github-actions.md is GA with complete example workflows in this monorepo's claude-code-actions/ subrepo (7 workflows). The maturity gap is the single strongest signal in this decision.
/code-review skill and code.claude.com/docs/en/code-review docs assume `gh pr diff` upstream. claude-code-security-review/ already ships scanners wired to GH Actions.
/ultrareview takes a GitHub PR number; ultraplan docs show GitHub repo/PR URLs. Works without a remote for the local-branch variant, but the networked form is GH.
First-party GitHub MCP server is already enabled in this repo (mcp__github__* used extensively: list_pull_requests, add_issue_comment, pull_request_review_write, etc.). Zero switching cost.
Computer-use operates on any web UI, but github.com's stable selectors + existing Claude examples give it a small edge in recipe availability vs gitlab.com.

code.claude.com/docs/en/github-enterprise-server.md documents the integration, and user already has an active GitHub Enterprise (enterprises/subagentskills) with the managedsubagents org ready. Zero procurement friction.

9 subrepos including claude-code-actions/, devops-review/, and claude-code-security-review/ are all GitHub-native. CLAUDE.md references `gh pr create`/GitHub Actions throughout. Switching would be a rewrite.

code.claude.com/docs/en/whats-new/rss.xml items consistently show GitHub-first integrations (github-actions, github-enterprise-server, web app PR support). GitLab CI/CD integration is maintained by GitLab per the beta doc.
docs.github.com/en/copilot/concepts/agents/anthropic-claude documents Copilot's Claude integration — strong validation signal at GitHub's side.
GitHub Enterprise per-seat is standard; Copilot is additive. Starts with existing subagentskills enterprise, so no new procurement at current scale.
No migration: jadecli/researchers is already on GitHub with 9 subrepos + submodules + PR history. Status-quo cost is zero.
claude-code-on-the-web.md does not document GitLab MR ingestion; workaround would require custom CI hooks pushing context to the web session.

code.claude.com/docs/en/gitlab-ci-cd.md states explicitly "currently in beta, features and functionality may evolve" and is maintained by GitLab, not Anthropic. Acceptable for exploration, not for production gating.
No Anthropic-authored GitLab /code-review recipe; feasible via glab CLI piped to `claude -p` but documentation and examples are thin.
ultraplan/ultrareview docs reference GitHub PR numbers. Manual bundling of a GitLab MR diff into the local-branch flow works but loses the networked form.
A community GitLab MCP server exists, but scope lags the first-party github MCP (no PR review write, no copilot review request). This repo would need to rewire every mcp__github__* call site.
gitlab.com is an equally well-known web UI; computer-use recipes would transfer with modest selector rework. Near-parity with github.com.
GitLab Self-Managed is mature, but code.claude.com docs currently do not cover a self-hosted GitLab integration the way github-enterprise-server.md covers GHES.

Every hook, subrepo workflow, and CLAUDE.md command in this monorepo targets GitHub. Switching would touch claude-code-actions/, devops-review/, .github/, and every mcp__github__* call — weeks of rewrites for no feature gain.
Per the gitlab-ci-cd.md note, GitLab maintains the integration. RSS changelog cadence for GitLab features therefore trails Anthropic's own releases.
claude.com/customers/gitlab and claude.com/customers/gitlab-enterprise show real production usage at GitLab itself — strong validation on the GitLab side.
GitLab bundles CI/CD minutes + integrated DevSecOps scanners in a single tier; at scale (10+ seats) the economics are mildly favorable vs separate Copilot.
Migrating 9 subrepos + submodules + PR history + CI workflows from GitHub is nontrivial. Doable but high-effort and reversible only with parallel mirroring.

recommendation.summary: Adopt GitHub as the primary host using the managedsubagents org under the existing subagentskills Enterprise. The integration maturity gap (GA vs beta CI, first-party MCP, Anthropic-authored docs for Enterprise Server, web app + ultrareview + code-review all GH-native) compounds with the zero-migration cost: the monorepo is already on GitHub and every subrepo's tooling targets it.
recommendation.main-tradeoff: GitLab has stronger bundled DevSecOps (SAST/DAST/dependency scanning) and a nicer CI/CD minutes economic model at larger team sizes, but those wins do not offset the cost of rewriting every Anthropic-integration path today.
recommendation.hedge: Keep gitlab.com/managedsubagents dormant as an escape valve. Revisit if (a) Anthropic promotes gitlab-ci-cd.md out of beta AND ships first-party GitLab MCP, or (b) Claude Code on the Web, ultraplan, and ultrareview add documented GitLab MR support. Mirror the decision report quarterly against rss.xml to catch changes.

next-actions.action: Move jadecli/researchers under the managedsubagents GitHub org (or fork it there) and flip the default remote — preserves git history and PR numbers.

Enable GitHub Actions workflows from claude-code-actions/ on the new org repo: claude-code-ci.yml (PR review), claude-code-security.yml (scanner triage), both using CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN from org secrets.
Wire Claude Code on the Web to the new repo: configure SessionStart hook to read .claude/bootstrap.xml, add org-level OAuth token, verify /ultrareview runs against a test PR.
Register the GitHub MCP server (already in use) for the new org scope and confirm mcp__github__* tool authorization.
Add a quarterly loop (/loop 90d) that re-runs this skill against refs/git-platform-decision-spec.xml so the decision stays rated against the current RSS feed.
Keep gitlab.com/managedsubagents group private with 0 projects as a documented fallback; add a reminder in the hedge section of this report to re-evaluate on beta-graduation.
